name: Cape Breton Oatcakes
recipeIngredient: 1/3 cup Shortening, or lard or bacon fat
recipeInstructions:
Sweet version: Combine the flour, oats, brown sugar, baking powder, and salt in a bowl. Mix in the shortening with your fingertips until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Gradually add cold water and mix until a dough forms. Divide the dough into two equal portions. On a floured surface, roll out each portion to about 1/4 inch (5 mm) thickness. Cut into squares approximately 2 1/2 inches (6 cm). Place on lightly greased baking sheets and bake in a preheated oven at 350°F (175°C) for 15 minutes or until lightly browned. Remove from the oven and let cool on wire racks.
Savory Variation: (to serve with cheese)
Combine the Scotch-type oatmeal, flour, granulated sugar, baking powder, and salt. Mix in the shortening, lard, or bacon fat. Gradually add cold water. Proceed as above by rolling out, cutting into squares, and baking on lightly greased sheets until browned.
